name: Lamorak
description: Sir Lamorak, son of King Pellinore, was a renowned knight of the Round Table in Arthurian legend. He was known for his exceptional strength, skill in combat, and fiery temper. Considered one of the strongest knights in the world, Lamorak was famed for his prowess in tournaments and battles. His lineage connected him to a family of powerful figures, including his brothers Percival and Aglovale, and his sister Dindrane. Lamorak's tragic fate is intertwined with the feud between his family and the family of Sir Gawaine. He was eventually ambushed and slain by Gawaine and his brothers (Agravaine, Gaheris, and Mordred) due to his affair with Morgause, Gawaine's mother and King Lot's wife. Lamorak's death is considered a significant loss to the Round Table, diminishing its strength and contributing to the eventual downfall of Camelot. His story highlights themes of loyalty, betrayal, honor, and the destructive nature of family feuds within the Arthurian world. Despite his flaws, Lamorak remains a compelling figure, embodying both the chivalric ideals and the darker aspects of the knightly code.
subtitle: The Fierce and Fated Knight
achievements: Being considered one of the strongest knights
Participating in many quests for the Round Table
Killing Sir Froll of the Out Isles

keyMyths.title: Affair with Morgause
keyMyths.description: Lamorak's affair with Morgause, the wife of King Lot and mother of Gawaine, led to his eventual demise. This transgression deeply offended Gawaine and his brothers, fueling their desire for revenge.
keyMyths.source: Le Morte d'Arthur by Thomas Malory

keyMyths.title: Ambush and Death
keyMyths.description: Lamorak was ambushed and murdered by Gawaine, Agravaine, Gaheris, and Mordred while unarmed. This act of treachery highlights the brutality and the breakdown of chivalry within the Arthurian court.
keyMyths.source: Le Morte d'Arthur by Thomas Malory

keyMyths.title: Son of Pellinore
keyMyths.description: As the son of King Pellinore, Lamorak inherited both strength and a connection to a family marked by conflict. His father's slaying by Sir Gawaine further intensified the animosity between the two families.
keyMyths.source: Le Morte d'Arthur by Thomas Malory

sources.author: Thomas Malory
sources.description: The definitive source for Arthurian legend, detailing Lamorak's exploits and tragic end.
sources.text: The definitive source for Arthurian legend, detailing Lamorak's exploits and tragic end.
